title: Per capita cheese consumption correlates with deaths by bedsheet tangling (r = 0.947)
description: Annual US per capita cheese consumption tracked the number of people who died by becoming tangled in their bedsheets at r = 0.947 from 2000–2009. Both variables grew over the same decade with no possible causal connection. A commonly cited counter-example to the naive claim that 'high correlation means something real', and a useful entry point for discussing confounders and ecological fallacies.
